rationale: The cited paper is used in the hypothesis-development section, not just in a passing literature list. Specifically, the citing paper relies on Slotegraaf et al. (2003) to support the claim that in high-intangibles industries firms face greater causal ambiguity in identifying inputs that build intangible assets. That claim is then used to motivate the customer-industry intangibles-intensity moderation logic (H5/H6), so the citation plays a meaningful conceptual role in the model framing.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as conceptual grounding for its argument about causal ambiguity in intangible-intensive industries. This support underpins the moderator hypotheses on how relationship multiplexity affects sales outcomes under varying customer-industry intangibles intensity.
